---
title: QMD Rendering Test - Please Delete
slug: qmd-rendering-test
author: Khuyen Tran
categories:
- Engineering
tags:
- test
meta_description: Learn how to use Quarto Markdown for WordPress blog posts with executable
  Python code blocks, syntax highlighting, and command-line rendering.
focus_keyword: quarto markdown wordpress
wordpress_url: https://openteams.com/qmd-rendering-test/
wordpress_id: 21836
last_synced: '2026-04-14T16:49:03Z'
---

## Quarto Code Blocks

Quarto uses `{python}` syntax for executable code blocks:

```{python}
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "name": ["Alice", "Bob", "Charlie"],
    "score": [95, 87, 92]
})
print(df.to_markdown(index=False))
```

## Hidden Code Block

This block has `echo: false` and should not appear in WordPress:

```{python}
#| echo: false
secret_config = {"api_key": "should-not-render"}
```

## Mixed with Standard Markdown

```python
#| highlight: 2-3
def greet(name: str) -> str:
    """Generate a greeting."""
    return f"Hello, {name}!"

print(greet("OpenTeams"))
```

## Bash Command-Line

```bash
#| command-line
#| data-filter-output: (out)
quarto render posts/qmd-rendering-test.qmd
(out)Output created: posts/qmd-rendering-test.html
```

## Table

| Language | Quarto Syntax | Standard Syntax |
|----------|--------------|-----------------|
| Python | `{python}` | `python` |
| R | `{r}` | `r` |
| Julia | `{julia}` | `julia` |
